title: uCAP
shortDescription: A Web App for Class Records and COAs.
detailedDescription: uCAP, or University Course Assessment Portal, is a web application that combines class records and Course Outcome Assessments (COA) into one platform. It is designed for USTP instructors to eliminate the need for separate spreadsheets and manual processes.

My main responsibilities include front-end development, with contributions to back-end development and database design. Designed a faculty-focused UI aligned with USTP branding. Developed RESTful APIs and handled data management for six user roles. Also implemented a PDF extraction feature for course syllabi. Then optimized both front-end and back-end performance to ensure efficient and responsive data processing.

title: uTrace
shortDescription: A Mobile App for Campus Navigation.
detailedDescription: uTrace, or University Trace, is a mobile application for campus navigation designed primarily for students, personnel, and visitors, who are unfamiliar with building routes and locations.

My main responsibilities for this project include front-end development and designing an intuitive, user-friendly interface, featuring a custom map design. Integrated Leaflet.js map API and implemented geolocation and pathfinding functionality.

title: HR System
shortDescription: A Web App UI Design for HRIS.
detailedDescription: The HR System is a task created for leave management for employees. It is only focused on one task flow for leave requests.
My main responsibilities for this project include UI/UX design, from analyzing the problem, defining user personas and task flows to creating low-, mid-, and high-fidelity interfaces.

title: YAHSHUA Compass
shortDescription: A Brand Design for an Internship Management System.
detailedDescription: YAHSHUA Compass is a project for an internship management system. It is designed to alleviate pain points experienced by the supervisors handling internships at YAHSHUA.

My main responsibilities for this project include requirements analysis and end-to-end brand design for Yahshua Compass, including visual identity, design guidelines, typography, color system, and logo.

title: Onotes
shortDescription: A Web App UI Design for Note-Taking.
detailedDescription: Onotes is a web application designed for efficient and simplified note-taking. This project is insipired from QuickNotes.
My main responsibilities include UI/UX design and front-end development, with a focus on implementing a browser-only storage for notes, without the need for a backend or database.

title: BarakoCafé
shortDescription: An eCommerce Website for Coffee Shop.
detailedDescription: BarakoCafé is an eCommerce website with the theme for a nonexistent coffee shop. It is designed and developed primarily to operate as a platform for selling coffee and related products.

My main responsibilities include front-end development and UI/UX design only, excluding back-end and database work. Which means no functional capabilities like an actual eCommerce platform. Built while learning web development, focusing on a visually appealing, user-friendly interface aligned with the BarakoCafé theme. Implemented responsive design for compatibility across devices and screen sizes.

title: Yorushika
shortDescription: A Playlist Website for Yorushika Fans.
detailedDescription: Yorushika is a playlist website designed for fans such as myself. It allows browsing to some of the songs by the artist.

My main responsibilities include front-end development and UI/UX design only, excluding back-end and database work. Built while learning web development, focusing on a visually appealing, user-friendly interface aligned with the Yorushika theme. Also an exploration for video, audio, and captions embedding capabilities in web development.
